title: Standardize empty state copy across watchlist & data panels
body:
Empty state messaging lacks consistency. The watchlist uses 'Empty. Add a ticker above.' while TopMovers uses 'No data', and medication tracker uses 'No medications logged.' This creates cognitive friction and doesn't guide users toward next actions consistently.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/Watchlist.tsx:121 — 'Empty. Add a ticker above.'
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/TopMovers.tsx:48, 71 — 'No data' (appears twice, not actionable)
- src/renderer/src/modules/health/MedicationTracker.tsx:113 — 'No medications logged.'

**Problems:**
1. Messaging varies in tone (imperative vs passive vs descriptive)
2. TopMovers 'No data' doesn't explain why (no watchlist items?)
3. No pattern guides the user toward the first action
4. Accessibility: low semantic clarity for screen readers

**Recommendations:**
- Establish template: '[Context] is empty. [Primary action here].'
- Make it action-oriented: 'No stocks in your watchlist yet. Add your first ticker above.'
- Use consistent placeholder visuals (icon + copy + button CTA)
- Ensure helper text explains *why* the section exists

title: Inconsistent loading state copy creates UX ambiguity
body:
Loading messages vary significantly in specificity and tone, causing users to wonder if the app is hung or actively fetching. Some lazy ('Loading…'), some specific ('Loading yield curve…'), some absent entirely.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/screener/ScreenerPage.tsx:118 — 'Loading…' (generic, 80+ rows of data)
- src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:114 — 'Loading…' (no context)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/YieldCurve.tsx:36 — 'Loading yield curve…' (specific, good)

**Problems:**
1. Specificity varies: users don't know what's loading in generic cases
2. Duration expectations unclear: 'Loading…' gives no time estimate
3. 'YieldCurve' example is good but not used elsewhere
4. No escape hatch if stuck (cancel button pattern missing)

**Recommendations:**
- Adopt specific copy: 'Loading [thing]…' not bare 'Loading…'
- Add time estimate for slow operations (5-30s range)
- Implement cancel button for long-running fetches
- Use consistent spinner + copy layout across app

title: Error messages lack actionability and user-friendly tone
body:
Error messages use passive construction ('Scoreboard failed', 'Standings failed') and expose raw technical details instead of explaining problems in user language or suggesting recovery steps.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:110, 207 — 'Scoreboard failed:' / 'Standings failed:' (passive, appends raw error.message)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/StockDetail.tsx — 'Fundamentals load failed:' (similarly uninformed)
- src/renderer/src/shared/ErrorBoundary.tsx:36, 40 — Crashes show raw Error.message in monospace (no glossary)

**Problems:**
1. 'Failed' is vague—users don't know if it's network, API key, or bad data
2. Raw error messages leak jargon (e.g., 'CORS error', 'HTTP 429')
3. No recovery action suggested (retry, check settings, contact support)
4. Error colors + icons exist but not all errors use them

**Recommendations:**
- Categorize errors: network vs auth vs rate-limit vs bad-input
- Translate to plain language: 'We couldn't reach the scoreboard. Check your internet and try again.'
- Include actionable next step in every error
- Link to help docs for complex errors
- Avoid raw exception text in user-facing UI

title: Button labels lack verb consistency and context
body:
Button copy varies in pattern and specificity. Some say 'Save', others 'Save alerts', others 'Save note'—creating inconsistency on when to be explicit vs generic. Missing secondary actions (Cancel, Reset) clarity.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ApiKeyManager.tsx:121 — 'Save' (key pair)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/alerts/AlertsModal.tsx:150 — 'Save alerts' (explicit)
- src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/MeetingNotes.tsx:176 — 'Save note' (explicit)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/Watchlist.tsx:109-114 — Unnamed button with Plus icon only, no label
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/TradeForm.tsx:105 — 'Save' (for trade, implicit)

**Problems:**
1. Generic 'Save' on critical forms (API keys, trades) lack specificity
2. Some buttons have no text labels, only icons (accessibility fail)
3. Cancel/Reset buttons sometimes have tooltips, sometimes don't
4. 'Save alerts' vs 'Save note' inconsistent pattern

**Recommendations:**
- Use pattern: verb + object when object not obvious from context
- Primary buttons: 'Save [X]' (e.g., 'Save API Key', 'Save Trade')
- Icon-only buttons must have aria-label + title tooltip
- Destructive secondaries: 'Delete [X]' not generic 'Delete'
- Add loading states: 'Saving…' (show work)

title: Form field placeholders used as labels, missing helper text
body:
Critical form fields lack associated labels and helper text, relying only on placeholder text which disappears on focus. Users don't understand *why* fields matter, especially in financial/health contexts.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/health/MedicationTracker.tsx:75 — placeholder='2x daily' (what is frequency? doctor's orders? user routine?)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/TradeForm.tsx:69-83 — 'Qty', 'Price', 'Fees' have no labels (no helper text explaining decimal places, validation)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/Watchlist.tsx:103 — placeholder='AAPL' (ticker format not explained)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/alerts/AlertsModal.tsx:88, 118 — placeholder='—' (bad UX, use label instead)

**Problems:**
1. Placeholder text disappears on focus, violating WCAG 2.1 label requirements
2. No validation hints (e.g., 'Price in dollars only')
3. For health/finance, field purpose unclear (why does this matter?)
4. Accessibility: screen readers don't announce placeholder as label

**Recommendations:**
- Always pair input with `<label>` tag
- Use placeholder only for format hint ('e.g., AAPL'), not label
- Add helper text below each field: 'Take once daily with food' or 'Fractional shares allowed'
- Clearly mark required fields
- For financial fields, specify decimal places: 'Price (e.g., 123.45 USD)'

title: Financial jargon lacks tooltips and definitions
body:
App uses financial terminology (Yield curve inversion, P/E, Fwd P/E, Mkt Cap, EPS) without definitions, tooltips, or glossary. New users can't understand screens; power users aren't slowed down by explanations.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/YieldCurve.tsx:79 — title='Yield curve inversion (5Y > 10Y)...' (good, but buried in title attribute)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/screener/ScreenerPage.tsx:127-135 — Table headers 'P/E', 'Fwd P/E', 'Mkt Cap' have no tooltips
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/detail/panels/FinancialRatios.tsx — (referenced in filename, likely similar issue)

**Problems:**
1. Wall of acronyms on Screener page (P/E, Fwd P/E, EPS, Mkt Cap, Vol)
2. No glossary or help link
3. Tooltips only on yield curve, inconsistent elsewhere
4. New traders see jargon with no context

**Recommendations:**
- Add title='...' to all financial abbreviations
- Create inline glossary modal: 'What does P/E mean?'
- Link column headers to help docs for complex metrics
- Use full names on first occurrence per screen: 'Price-to-Earnings Ratio (P/E)'
- Add context-sensitive help icon (?) next to advanced panels

title: Delete/destructive actions lack confirmation copy and UX patterns
body:
Buttons that remove data (delete medication, remove from watchlist, delete trade) lack confirmation dialogs or destructive-action phrasing. Users can accidentally lose data without warning.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/health/MedicationTracker.tsx:147 — Delete button with no confirmation, icon-only
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/Watchlist.tsx:199 — Remove button (X icon, title='Remove', no confirm)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/TradesTable.tsx — (likely has delete, unconfirmed)
- src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ApiKeyManager.tsx:143 — Delete key (Trash2 icon, no confirm)

**Problems:**
1. No confirmation dialog appears before deletion
2. Destructive buttons don't use distinctive styling (red/neg color good, but no undo message)
3. Icon-only destructive buttons fail accessibility
4. No undo or recovery message after deletion

**Recommendations:**
- Show confirmation modal: 'Delete medication [name]? This cannot be undone.'
- Use clear destructive verb: 'Delete' not 'Remove'
- Add aria-label to icon buttons: 'Delete medication'
- Show success toast after deletion: 'Deleted. Undo?' (if feasible)
- For irreversible actions, use two-step confirm: button → modal → final confirm

title: First-time user experience lacks onboarding copy and inline guidance
body:
New users see empty screens or generic CTAs without understanding the app's value or how to get started. No tooltips on first interaction or progressive disclosure.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/Watchlist.tsx:121 — 'Empty. Add a ticker above.' (assumes user knows what to do)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/MarketSummary.tsx:64 — 'AI-generated summary... Requires configured AI key.' (late discovery of requirement)
- src/renderer/src/modules/_placeholders/ComingSoon.tsx:9 — 'Coming soon.' (no ETA, no value prop)

**Problems:**
1. New users don't know watchlist is a required first step
2. AI feature requirements discovered only after clicking
3. Coming Soon panels lack feature description or mailing list signup
4. No tour or ?/help icon on complex screens

**Recommendations:**
- Add onboarding modal for first use: 'Your watchlist is where you track stocks you care about.'
- Use progressive disclosure: show help text only if section stays empty 5+ seconds
- For Coming Soon features, show: 'Coming in Q2 2026. [Sign up for updates]'
- Add help icon (?) on complex screens that opens tooltips + video
- Use Launchpad as true quickstart, not just module switcher

title: Loading state copy in YieldCurve should be replicated for pattern consistency
body:
YieldCurve's 'Loading yield curve…' is the only specific, well-scoped loading message. This good pattern isn't replicated in similar data panels, creating inconsistency.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/YieldCurve.tsx:36 — 'Loading yield curve…' (exemplary)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/CryptoTracker.tsx — (likely generic 'Loading')
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/detail/panels/IndicatorsPanel.tsx — (likely missing)

**Problems:**
1. One good example buried; not surfaced as pattern
2. Data panels vary in loading copy
3. No loading pattern guide for new components

**Recommendations:**
- Create loading copy guide in component library or design tokens
- Adopt: 'Loading [panel name]…' as standard
- Create LoadingPlaceholder component with branded placeholder
- Document in Storybook or CONTRIBUTING.md

title: Input placeholder misuse in alerts modal + API key field
body:
Placeholder='—' in AlertsModal price inputs is unclear (what does '—' mean as placeholder?). API key input uses context to explain, but should be more explicit.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/alerts/AlertsModal.tsx:88, 118 — placeholder='—' (unclear, should be 'Optional' or 'None')
- src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ApiKeyManager.tsx:103 — placeholder='Paste [name] API key' (good, but could clarify: 'Paste your... API key')

**Problems:**
1. '—' is a glyph, not helpful text
2. 'Paste' imperative is slightly jarring
3. No indication that fields are optional

**Recommendations:**
- Replace '—' with 'Optional' or 'Leave blank to remove alert'
- Use consistent placeholder pattern: 'Paste your [Provider] API key here'
- Add explicit label: 'Alert when price ≥' already exists, good

title: Inconsistent CTA phrasing (Generate w/ AI, Summarize, AI Score All, etc.)
body:
AI action buttons use different patterns: 'Generate w/ AI', 'Summarize with AI', 'AI Score All'. Inconsistent capitalization and verb choice creates mental overhead.

**Issue locations:**
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/MarketSummary.tsx:45 — 'Generate w/ AI' (abbreviated)
- src/renderer/src/modules/finance/screener/ScreenerPage.tsx:108 — 'AI Score All' (noun-first)
- src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/MeetingNotes.tsx:161 — 'Summarize with AI' (verb-first, no abbrev)

**Problems:**
1. Different capitalization ('w/ AI', 'with AI', 'AI')
2. Pattern varies: verb+noun vs noun+verb
3. 'Score All' is jargon, unclear without context
4. Users see multiple AI patterns, not one brand voice

**Recommendations:**
- Standardize: 'Action with AI' or 'AI: Action' pattern
- Choose: 'Generate', 'Analyze', 'Summarize' (consistent verbs)
- Avoid abbreviations: use 'with' not 'w/'
- Examples: 'Analyze with AI', 'Summarize with AI', 'Generate summary with AI'
- Create AI CTA style guide in design tokens
